description: Current-cluster Calico + private sandbox egress foundation for personal-ai-gke (ADR-146 Slice 0.1). Dataplane V2 migration is outside this ADR.

maintenancePolicy.implication: No maintenance window is configured: enabling NetworkPolicy can begin recreating every node pool immediately.

calicoOwnedDenies.reason: These destinations can be required or transformed inside the node datapath. VPC firewall denial could break cross-node kube-dns/Squid or cannot reliably observe the final destination; mandatory Calico policies own them.

unclaimedActiveProbes: inbound denial from an external source Pod/listener

scope.gkeSnatTruth: Default GKE masquerading changes public Pod traffic to the node primary IP. NAT therefore selects the cluster subnet PRIMARY_IP_RANGE plus the dedicated sandbox Pod secondary. Cluster-wide 0.0.0.0/0 nonMasquerade and disable-default-snat are forbidden.
scope.attributionClaim: The static NAT identity is currently sandbox-exclusive only while structural live verification proves every eligible no-external-IP VM/node in the regional VPC inventory belongs to sandbox-pool-private.

honestLimitations: VPC firewall cannot reliably see node-local metadata (169.254.169.254); Calico/NetworkPolicy remains mandatory for metadata denial.
VPC firewall cannot reliably see Service ClusterIP traffic after kube-proxy/iptables DNAT on the node; Calico/NetworkPolicy remains mandatory for Service denial.
Same-node Pod traffic that never leaves the host datapath is outside VPC firewall visibility; Calico/NetworkPolicy remains mandatory.

sandboxNodePool.name: sandbox-pool
notes: Do not pass sandbox.gke.io/runtime in --node-labels; GKE manages that label and rejects manual specification (HTTP 400).
Inventory labels still expect the resulting GKE-managed runtime label for live exact match.
Same-key scheduling taint remains on create via --node-taints; GKE Sandbox docs apply it with sandbox, and gcloud does not document label-style rejection for that taint key.
Enabling GKE NetworkPolicy (Calico) on LEGACY_DATAPATH recreates all node pools.
If a maintenance window/exclusion is active, nodes may not recreate until the window; operators must wait or manually upgrade.
Helm networkPolicy.enabled only renders API objects; it does not enable the Calico engine.

allowedActions: Recreate the private sandbox node pool with the same least-privilege SA, private nodes, labels, taints, and network tags.
Repair Cloud NAT / static addresses / firewall deny rules without removing them.
Scale the private sandbox pool while keeping Calico enabled.
Product rollback remains set-all-assistants-to-restricted after S1+; never re-enable the removed plan networkAccessEnabled boolean.
forbiddenActions: Moving sandbox workloads back onto public-IP / Editor-identity nodes
Deleting the private-egress deny firewall without a replacement deny

residuals: Human Environment approval on persai-dev-adr146-foundation is still required after the sandbox-only pin and founder-owned live structural/active verification.
When a migration is co-present, persai-dev-adr146-foundation approval must precede persai-dev-migrations; neither Environment may be bypassed.
plan/verify/generate-probe-manifests/probe-restricted refuse dirty trees and disk≠commit inventory mismatches; evidence never emits UNAVAILABLE.
Controlled probe Pods require explicit `cleanup-controlled-probes --execute` after use (success or failure); they are not auto-cleaned and must not be left indefinitely.

Live parent evidence (founder-approved preflight/apply/verify/probe-restricted bound to git commit SHA + inventory SHA-256) remains outside CI; CI does not auto-apply foundation mutations or fabricate GKE attestation.
pushLastSequence: pre-push founder-approved foundation apply (preflight → apply → retire → structural verify prerequisites)
one final founder push of the coordinated ADR-146 commit range from a clean tree
Argo syncs Helm KSA/NetworkPolicy from HEAD while non-sandbox image tags remain last-good
Dev Image Publish pins sandbox only after sandbox image build succeeds
operator applies controlled probe manifests + runs structural verify and probe-restricted, then cleanup-controlled-probes (required on success and failure)
when migrations are also present, approve persai-dev-migrations after the foundation Environment (ordered dual gate)
failureRollback: Remain on last-good non-sandbox image pins if verification fails
Sandbox tag may be rolled back independently
Never restore the removed plan networkAccessEnabled boolean
